sameDay.text: Welcome back! You're on a roll today.
Back for more? Let's keep training!
sameDay.jp: もっとれんしゅうしよう！

sameDay.text: You're doing great today. Keep it up!
Another round? I like your energy!
Still going strong! Let's do this.

streakAlive.text: You're back! Your streak lives on.
Right on time! Let's keep this streak alive.
The dojo is ready. Let's train!
streakAlive.jp: どうじょうへようこそ！

streakAlive.text: Welcome back! Your consistency is impressive.

streakAtRisk.text: Oh! I was starting to wonder about you. Good timing!
Close call! One more day and your streak would've been gone.
You made it just in time! Let's train while we're here.
I was worried! But you're here now. That's what matters.
Phew! Your streak was on the edge. Let's lock in today.

streakBroken.text: Hey! It's good to see you again. Ready for a fresh start?
Welcome back to the dojo! Every journey has pauses.
You're here and that's what counts. Let's build a new streak!
The best time to start again is right now. Let's go!
Missed you! No worries — pick up right where you left off.

streakGone.text: Rikizo stares out the window... 'They haven't come back...'
Rikizo has been wearing the same headband for days. In solidarity.
The dojo is quiet. Too quiet. Rikizo sweeps the floor alone.
Rikizo dropped his おにぎり in shock. It's been days?!
Rikizo checked the door 47 times today. Just saying.

streakDesperate.text: BREAKING: Local tanuki hasn't eaten in days. Claims student abandoned him.
Rikizo started a diary. Day 7: 'I wonder if they found another sensei...'
Rikizo put up a 'MISSING STUDENT' poster. It's very dramatic.
Rikizo tried to send a carrier pigeon. It came back confused.
streakDesperate.jp: てがみをおくったけど…

streakDesperate.text: The training dummies are gathering dust. Even they look sad.

streakAbsurd.text: Rikizo has started learning English out of spite. Please come back before it's too late.
streakAbsurd.jp: えいごをべんきょうしてる！

streakAbsurd.text: Rikizo auditioned for Duolingo. They said his guilt trips weren't aggressive enough.
streakAbsurd.jp: デュオリンゴにおうぼした…

streakAbsurd.text: URGENT: Rikizo has formed a one-tanuki protest outside your phone. He has tiny signs.
Rikizo wrote a haiku about waiting: 'Empty dojo floor / Headband collecting the dust / Please come back to me'
Rikizo is knitting you a 'Please Study' sweater. He's on sleeve two.

morning.text: Good morning! A fresh day for kanji.
Morning study hits different. Let's go!

afternoon.text: Afternoon! Let's keep the momentum going.
Good afternoon — a little practice now?
Afternoon break? Perfect time for a lesson.

evening.text: Welcome back this evening. Let's wind down with some kanji.
Evening session — your future self will thank you.

night.text: Up late? A little practice, then get some rest.
Night owl, huh? Let's do a quick one.
Late-night kanji — just don't overdo it!

encouragement.text: Little by little — that's how fluency is built.

greeting.jp: はじめまして！わたしはりきぞうだよ。

intro.jp: いっしょににほんごをべんきょうしましょう！

praiseYes.text: Great — your Japanese is good enough for this app.

praiseNo.text: No worries — you may want more hiragana practice, but you're welcome to try!

tourNotify.text: This bell has your notification settings. Turn them on and I will remind you every day and when your streak is in trouble.

tourSettingsYes.text: The gear is your settings — set up your account, pick your preferences, and (once Adventure unlocks) collect character stamps here. And if reading ever feels tricky, there are aids in here to make it easier. Just pop in whenever you need a hand.

tourSettingsNo.text: The gear is your settings — your account, preferences, and character stamps later on. Since a little extra reading help can't hurt, let me open it up and show you a few aids.

settingsFurigana.text: Furigana puts tiny hiragana above each kanji, so you can read a word before you've learned its kanji. Flip this on whenever you want the support.

settingsRomaji.text: Romaji spells the Japanese out in the Latin alphabet underneath. Handy at the very start — just try to lean on it less as your kana get stronger.

settingsKana.text: And Kana Writing Practice adds stroke-by-stroke drills for hiragana and katakana — the quickest way to stop needing those aids at all. Turn it on and practice anytime.

tourStreak.text: This is your training streak. Your belt advances as you train — miss a day and a streak freeze covers you, but miss two in a row and you'll fall back a belt until you are back at white.

tourLessons.text: And this is your next task to advance — score 60% or higher to unlock more.

passedOpening.text: Nice job on {LESSON}! That's a real step forward.

failedOpening.text: Good try on {LESSON}. Don't worry about the score — let's keep practicing.
Hey, you finished {LESSON}. The score will come with practice.
You showed up for {LESSON} — that's the hardest part. Let's sharpen it up.

unlockIntro.text: And look — you unlocked some new things. Let me show you!

passedClosing.text: Good job! You unlocked a lot — it's time to go explore and learn. I'll help you get used to each of them when you are ready. In the mean time, I'll be here the whole time.

tryAgainPrompt.text: Hit the Dojo or Grammar to practice, then try the lesson again — you'll pass next time.

noUnlocksPassed.text: Keep that streak alive — more will unlock as you go.

lesson.text: More lessons live here. Finish each one to unlock the next.

grammar.text: Grammar Garden — short lessons on how Japanese sentences glue together.

practice.text: The Dojo — flashcards and quizzes to lock in what you've learned.

compose.text: Compose — your own writing space. Try building sentences.

story.text: Stories — short readings that put what you know into the wild.

review.text: Review — refresh older lessons before they fade.

game.text: Adventure — explore Japan as Rikizo. Coming soon!

dojoHighScore.text: New Dojo best — {BEST} in a row! Your kanji are getting sharp.
{BEST} correct in a row in the Dojo! Personal best.
Look at that — {BEST}-streak in the Dojo. Keep that practice habit going.

intro.text: Welcome to your first lesson! Each page covers something different. Don't try to memorize it all in one go — repetition does the work. Pass the drills at the end with 60% to unlock what's next.

Here's how lessons work: each one introduces new vocab and a sprinkle of grammar in context. The grammar gets its proper deep-dive in Grammar Garden, and you'll practice it all in the Dojo and the other paths.
Up next is the Warmup — just a couple of quick questions to ease into the lesson.

warmup.text: Warmup questions — ask them out loud, to yourself or a partner. The first lesson keeps them basic, things you should already be able to say. Future lessons use them to reinforce vocab you've already learned. After this, we'll meet the new kanji for the lesson. (Down the road, when AI Rikizo is ready, you'll be able to drill these with me.)

kanjiGrid.text: These are the new kanji for this lesson. You can see its meaning, an example, and its readings. Some kanji have multiple meanings and most have multiple readings.

Most kanji have two reading types: ON (the Chinese-derived reading, used in compounds like 先生 = sensei) and KUN (the native Japanese reading, used when the kanji stands alone or with hiragana like 生まれる = umareru). Some kanji even have multiple On and Kun readings.

vocabList.text: Core vocab for this lesson — tap any word to hear it spoken. (If the lesson also introduces a new grammar piece, it'll live here too.) Don't try to memorize it all in one pass; you'll see these again in the conversations, readings, and drills. Complete the drills and unlock the Dojo to train more!

vocabListAssumed.text: Heads up — this page isn't a regular part of lessons. It's a one-time stop-gap to level the playing field for users coming in at different points. If this is all familiar, just scroll through. If not, take a minute to skim.

conversation.text: Conversations put the words in action. Tap any bubble to hear the line spoken; tap a single underlined word to pop up its reading, meaning, and (for verbs) its conjugation. Hit 'Show English' if you need the translation.

reading.text: Tap any line in the passage to reveal the English; tap any underlined word for its reading, meaning, and conjugation — same as in the conversations. Try reading each line aloud first.
After the reading, you'll get comprehension questions; answer them out loud in Japanese. (Down the road, AI Rikizo will be able to listen in and check your answers.)

drillsKanji.text: First drill: kanji readings. Each question gives you a kanji and asks for its よみかた (yomikata, 'how to read'). Pick the correct reading. Score 60% across all three drills to unlock the next lesson.

drillsVocab.text: Second drill: vocabulary. Each sentence has a blank — pick the word that fits best.

drillsSentence.text: Third drill: building sentences. Same blank-fill format, but the choices are particles (は, が, を, …). Pick the one that makes the sentence grammatical.

grammarIntro.text: Welcome to your first Grammar lesson! Grammar Garden goes deep on one pattern at a time — rules, examples, then a quick practice. This first page runs you through what the lesson will cover. Take your time; there's no rush.

grammarRule.text: Here we break down the grammar point covered. We deep dive into how to use it properly — and sometimes when not to use it, for cultural reasons.
Open up the Notes section to get extra tips and pointers, then practice the examples to gain a better understanding of how to use the grammar introduced.

grammarTable.text: Some grammar points are easier to compare in a chart! You'll occasionally run into these along the path — study them well to understand the differences.

grammarComparison.text: Similarly, some grammar points have closely-related forms that make more sense side-by-side. Comparison cards put two related forms next to each other so you can see when each fits — and how the tone shifts between them.

annotatedExample.text: Real examples to practice the various grammar points taught so far.

conversation.text: Same as in lessons: tap any bubble to hear it, tap an underlined word for its reading and meaning. Hit 'Show English' if you need the translation.

sentenceTransform.text: Your first interactive practice — take the source sentence and pick the right transformation. Each grammar lesson uses different interactive drills like this one. You'll need to complete it before moving on.

drills.text: Final round of practice. Pick the best answer; explanations come after each. Score 60% or higher to put Grammar 1 in the bank and unlock the next one.

complete.text: Congrats — you just finished your first Grammar lesson!
And just so you know… I'll be here the whole time. 👋

intro.text: Welcome to the Dojo! This is where you sharpen everything — vocab, kanji readings, verbs, and sentence structure. Mix and match whatever you want to drill.

stats.text: Up top: your Kanji / Vocab / Verbs / Flags count, all based on lessons you've completed. Tap them to pick which lessons to focus on — useful when you want to drill just a few.

kanjiPractice.text: Kanji Practice covers the basics — Flashcards flip through what you've learned. Meaning Quiz tests English↔Kanji. Reading Quiz tests Reading↔Kanji. Mix mode shuffles directions.

vocabPractice.text: Vocab Practice does the same but with compound words built from your kanji. Flashcards for review, Quiz for testing.

verbPractice.text: The Conjugation Station drills verb forms — polite, casual, past, te-form, all of it. Locked for now — Grammar 1 will open it once you've got the copula down.

verbPracticeUnlocked.text: The Conjugation Station drills verb forms — polite, casual, past, te-form, all of it. Nice — Grammar 1 already unlocked it for you.

sentencePractice.text: Sentence Practice puts everything in motion. Scramble re-orders sentences for you to rebuild. Link Up sorts words into groups. Both unlock as your vocab grows.

flagged.text: Anything you got wrong in a drill gets flagged automatically — Review Flagged lets you hammer just those weak spots. Use it often.

levelPicker.text: Welcome to Compose! This is where you build your own sentences in Japanese. Pick a level to see compositions tied to your lessons.

lessonMenu.text: Each card here is a different composition using words from your last lesson.
There are prompts for what to write and word banks for help. They get longer the further down the path you go!
Don't worry though — they auto-save, so you can take your time and keep your progress.

prompt.text: This is prompt 1 — it gives you a direction to start the first segment of your composition.

targetWords.text: These are the target words for this segment of your composition.

wordBank.text: This is the word bank — helper words for when you're having trouble thinking of vocabulary to use.

particles.text: These are particles and copula you've learned (or are about to!) in case you need them.

conjugation.text: These are conjugation patterns you've learned (or are also about to!) in case you need a bump.

typeDemo.text: When you've completed all target words for a prompt, you can proceed to the next prompt.
…but hopefully your sentence is more complete than mine!

charCount.text: This shows characters written / the target for this composition.
Length is part of your score — hit the target to max out the Length points. The target grows as a composition adds more prompts.

score.text: After you've completed all prompts, press Score. We grade vocabulary use, sentence length, and grammar — each scored separately.

listen.text: If you want to hear how your composition sounds, tap Listen.

clear.text: Clear wipes your composition and starts over — there's no undo, so use it carefully.

wrapUp.text: That's it — good luck! I'll be here the whole time. 👋
